Brewster, General Reindeer Superintendent, Alaska Ranger Service; Wm. M. S. Ivanoff teacher from Nash Harbor, Nunivak Island (Interpreter).

People: 1350: The Motor launch with the following persons aboard left for Etolin Point: Lieutenant Commander W. G. Bloom, in charge; J. F. Mace (106-619) CMM; Eric Lapeg, BM 1c; Walter Vickery (110-619) CRM; and Department of Interior Inspecting Party consisting of: Mr. Roy Nash; Lyman S. Brewster; and Mr. M. S. Ivanoff; and Assistant Surgeon Jack G. Mearns, U.S.P.H.S. The above party left for the purpose of observing the reindeer herd and range lands; investigating local conditions in native villages at Etolin Point.; and to render medical aid to the natives.

Flora&Fauna: 1640: Enroute to Cape Etolin, the party landed to observe a reindeer herd close at hand; the number was estimated to be over a thousand reindeer. Four such hers were seen and were observed to be in excellent condition.
